payload.answer: Great—knowledge graphs are structured representations of information as entities (nodes) connected by relationships (edges). What would you like to focus on: (1) basics/concepts, (2) how to build one, (3) query languages (e.g., SPARQL/Cypher), or (4) tools/learning resources? If you tell me your background (e.g., beginner/intermediate) and your goal (personal learning vs. a project), I can tailor a learning path.

entity_resolution.reason: The user expresses a general desire to learn; no stable personal preference, profile data, or future-use facts are provided.

details: Captured user request as an open learning intent without committing durable memory.

repair_options: Ask a follow-up question about preferred focus area
Request user's background and intended use case to tailor next steps
